title: Schoenus polycephalus
taxonomy: Schoenus polycephalus is classified within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family consists of approximately 5,000 species of plants, many of which are found in wetland environments. Schoenus polycephalus is further categorized within the genus Schoenus, which comprises around 50 species of sedges. These plants are typically found in freshwater or brackish environments and are known for their distinctive triangular stem and scale-like leaves. The exact classification of Schoenus polycephalus can be further refined within the subgenus Schoenus or the subgenus Lepidosperma, depending on the species.

intro: Schoenus polycephalus, commonly known as the "broad-leaved bog-rush," is a species of sedge that grows in wet environments, characterized by its broad, flat leaves and multiple heads of spikelets. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Schoenus polycephalus include phenolic compounds, terpenoids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory and skin issues.
